What live data covers

Live mode reads the public LACCD schedule and program map: the full 2-year schedule plus rotation, single-section and waitlist signals. Enrollment / capacity counts are not published by any LACCD API, so capacity / fill-rate analysis needs those numbers — add an enrollment export on the live form (experimental), or use a workbook that already includes them.

What this live build can measure

These depend on data the public schedule API does not include. An empty result here means “not yet measurable,” not “all clear.”

how: fill-rate computed on 4 of 6 sections

how: applied on the program path

some sections have no posted meeting time
to enable: supply day/time on every section
✓ First-year gateway momentum — on
checks whether each program's English/Math gateway can be SCHEDULED in the first year — an OFFERING proxy
✓ Corequisite co-availability — on
checks whether a gateway's catalog corequisite is scheduled in the SAME first-year term — a co-OFFERING STRUCTURE proxy

✓ Met by your major — areas 2A / 5A: BIOLOGY 3, MATH 261

Why this matters — research evidence

Sector-wide research evidence — published findings from OTHER institutions that explain WHY a flagged structural condition plausibly matters. This is NOT a measurement of this campus and NOT a prediction of this campus's outcomes.

  • 30%→43%; 9%→~18%; 23.7%→34.8% — Guided-pathway course maps with structured, data-informed advising raised graduation rates: Central Arizona College 30%→43%, Community College of Philadelphia 9%→~18% (doubled), and Bluegrass / BCTC 23.7%→34.8%.Source: Central Arizona College, Community College of Philadelphia, Bluegrass Community & Technical College; reported via Community College Daily and CCRC
  • 57% of students affected — 57% of students report having to spend more time and money because required courses are not offered at the times they need them.Source: Student-survey finding aggregated in the completion-research review (ScienceDirect / Community College Daily source set)
  • +2.3–2.8pp stop-out (22–28% relative) — Being shut out of a required course raises the probability of enrolling in no classes at all that term by 2.3–2.8 percentage points — a 22–28% relative increase over a roughly 10% baseline stop-out rate.Source: Robles et al., 'The effect of course shutouts on community college students,' ScienceDirect
  • +5.41pp persistence (+7.39pp new students) — Scheduling tools that reduce conflicts and increase course availability raised persistence by an average of 5.41 percentage points (up to 7.39 points for new students).Source: Completion-research review of scheduling-technology studies (Community College Daily / SAGE persistence study set)
  • Odessa: +12pp retention / +13% credentials; Kilgore: 26%→33% — Eight-week terms and standardized scheduling blocks improved outcomes: Odessa College in-class retention +12 percentage points and credentials awarded +13%; Kilgore College 150% graduation rate 26%→33%.Source: Odessa College, Kilgore College; reported via Community College Daily

Admin briefing

Two programs analyzed. Biology AS-T finishes full-time in 4 terms. MATH 227 is the top cross-program bottleneck — add a section.
Generated by edgesched · Schedule Planner. Live data is from LACCD’s public class-schedule API and Program Mapper; the analysis is section- and aggregate-level only (no student-level records). Prerequisite / GE coverage is a planning aid — confirm with a counselor.
